title: Malaxis palustris
intro: Malaxis palustris, also known as Marsh Orchid or Lesser Marsh Orchid, is a perennial plant species belonging to the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which have been attributed to its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Malaxis palustris include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Malaxis palustris is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits, particularly in traditional Chinese and Ayurvedic medicine.
taxonomy: Malaxis palustris is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This orchid is further classified within the subfamily Orchidoideae and tribe Orchideae. The genus Malaxis is part of the larger group of orchids known as the subfamily Orchidoideae. Malaxis palustris is closely related to other species within the genus Malaxis, which are commonly known as the "Adder's-mouth orchids". The classification of Malaxis palustris reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.
